def: n. f. 1. Want of credit; discredit. 2. Untrustworthiness; faithlessness. 3. Distrust; disbelief; suspiciousness.
adj. 1. Of no credit or estimation; not trustworthy; incredible. 2. Not to be trusted; unreliable; faithless. 3. Disbelieving; suspicious; incredulous; sceptical.
s. f. Freedom from care.
s.m. & adv. Speed, celerity, haste, rapidity; violence, impetuosity; — dung; — with haste, speedily, quickly, soon.

n. m. A title affixed to the names of Mogals, corresponding with lord, master, etc.
s. m. 1. Celerity, haste, rapidity; (adverbially) with haste, quickly, soon. 2. Dung.
s. m. A Moghal title, corresponding with the English, Lord.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بیگ (bēg), itself borrowed from Proto-Turkic *bēg via a Turkic language.

r: beg, imāla, bag, baga, bagha, bhaga
ety: by imāla for bag; Old P. baga; Zend bagha; S. bhaga
def: s.m. Lord, master; a Mog̠al title corresponding with the English Lord.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بیگ (bēg), itself borrowed from Proto-Turkic *bēg via a Turkic language.

def: s.f. Lady; queen: a title of Mogal ladies.
more: n. f. T. beg a lord, am a feminine affix. A title of Mogal ladies.
s. f. A lady, a queen. (Women of rank are called begams ). Plur. بيگمات or بيگمان.
wety: From Classical Persian بیگم (bēgum). Ultimately from Proto-Turkic.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Strange, foreign, another, not related, not domestic, not an acquaintance or friend, alien, unknown; — stranger, foreigner
more: adj. 1. Not related; foreign; strange; alien; unknown. 2. Not one's own; another's ( parāyā ). Chīj bigānī, māl birānā, Mat ghar apne lā manwāṅ, Ab tauṅ mat pāp kamā manwāṅ . Rus. song. Another's goods, another's property To bring within my house be far from me! Let not my soul with sin polluted be!

adj. Strange, not domestick, not an acquaintance or friend, foreign, unknown; stranger. بيگانه خو begāna-ḵẖo, Of an unfriendly or savage disposition. بيگانه وضع begāna-waẓﻌ, adj. Strange-mannered. بيگانه وضعي begāna-waẓʼīﻌ, s. f. Strangeness of manners.
subs: adj. Of an unfriendly, or savage disposition
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi बेगांनां (begā̃nā̃), from Classical Persian بیگانه (bēgāna). Compare Punjabi ਬਿਗਾਨਾ (bigānā) / بِگانہ (bigānā).

def: s.f. Strangeness, the being foreign or not domestic; estrangement; shyness.
more: s. f. Strangeness, the being foreign or not domestick.

def: s.m. Compelling to work for nothing; compulsory labour with or without pay; impressment of workmen, carriage, &c.; — a person forced to labour ( = be-gārī)
more: n. m. 1. Impressment of workmen, carriage, etc. 2. Forced labor with or without pay. 1. Chamār ko surag meṅ bhī begār . Prov. Even in Heaven forced labor is the cobbler's lot. 2. Khālī se be-gār bhalī . Prov. Better forced labor than do nothing. be-gār ṭālnā , v. n. To work carelessly. Kām karte ho, yā be-gār ṭālte ho?
s. m. A person forced to work with or without pay.
subs: v.t. To press into service with or without pay
v.n. To work in a half-hearted or perfunctory manner; to work carelessly.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بیگار (bē-gār, “forced labour”). By surface analysis, بے (be, “without”) + گار (gār, “doer”).

def: s.m. Gold tinsel; tin-foil; varnish.

selfless, unselfish, disinterested

more: n. m. A forced labourer; one pressed to carry burdens for individuals or the public.
s. f. The act of pressing or forcing to work. بيگاري لينا begārī lenā, To take forcibly or press, with or without pay.
subs: s.m. One compelled to work, a pressed man
v.t. To take forcibly (with or without pay), to press.

def: adj. & adv. = beg, q.v.; — s.m. Courier, express; a hawk. s.m. & adv. Speed, celerity, haste, rapidity; violence, impetuosity; — dung; — with haste, speedily, quickly, soon.
more: s. f. Celerity, haste, speed, precipitation.

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بیگناه (bē-gunāh).

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian به (ba).

def: s.m. Perforation, hole.
s. m. A hole, a perforation.
adj. 1. Damaged; unserviceable; worn out; used up; ruined. 2. Unemployed; badly off. Āj kal be-hāl haiṅ. be-hāl , adv. On the point of death. Be-hāl ho rahā hai so woh hāl kyā kare? Nazīr.
s.m. = bihān, q.v. s.m. Dawn, day-light, day-break, morning, morrow (syn. bhinsār)
subs: adv. At dawn, at day-break, in the morning, early, soon, betimes.

def: n. f. H. bīā seed. 1. A nursery for rice plants. 2. Advances given for seed grain.
s.f. Nursery for rice plants; advances given for seed-gram.
adj. & s.f. = behaṛ, q.v.
adj. Uneven, abrupt, rugged.

adj. & s.f. Uncultivated; uneven (land), rugged, rough, cut up, raviny; — broken ground, land broken into ravines, rugged or rough land or soil; sterile land, land not brought under cultivation, waste land; jungle, forest.
adj. Uneven; cut up; rugged; rough. be'haṛ, bihaṛ , n. f. 1. Rugged or rough land or soil; land broken into ravines. 2. A jungle; forest; thick forest. 3. Waste or sterile land; pasturage.

insensible, senseless, insensitive; callous
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بی حس.

def: (Pers. بيله) s. m. Money carried by great men to be distributed in charity when travelling, &c. بيـ‍ﮩ‍لابردار behlā-bardār, The carrier of the بيـﮩ‍لا. See P. بيلا.
s.m. corr. of P. bīlā, belā, q.v.
s.m. Carter (of cotton; = biknā or běhnā, q.v.). v.n. To be sold, to sell; — bik-jānā, v.n. intens. of, and = biknā; (met.) to be dependent (on), be obliged.
n. m. A carder ( dhunyā ). Be-dharmā bhaī, aur behnā ke sāth meṅ! Prov. Unchaste although I be, should I a carder wed of low degree?
more: v. n. S. वह to flow. 1. To flow; glide; slide; go or swim with the stream; run. 1. Aisī tere hī tale Gangā bahe hai? Prov. Oh yes! you can make the Ganges flow! (Said to a boaster). 2. Bahī teg kandhā, pare sīs niyāre, Gire ṭūṭ tarbūj se mund bhāre . Padmāwat. 2. To blow; waft; issue; gush; spout. Jaise bahe biyār, pīṭh tab taise dije . Prov. Turn your back to the wind. (Sail with the wind). 3. To run (as a sore); to discharge. 4. To become sloppy, watery, thin. 5. To melt; dissolve; liquify. 6. To be wasted, squandered, dissipated. 7. To be carried away; to be separated from or lost. 8. To be lost, ruined, destroyed. 9. To be sold off, disposed of, got rid of. 10. To be of no effect; fall through. 11. To be overlooked, or disregarded.
v. n. 1. To flow, to glide. 2. To float. 3. To blow. 4. To pass, drive.

def: s.m. Nur sery for rice plants ( = behan).
Rus. v. a. To foul; pollute. Kūtā pahṅḍā beho kar gayā rī, āyā nā Būjā kā bābū! W. Rus. song. The dog has fouled this vessel, the boy's father not yet come home!
s.m. Grass kept for pasturage.
n. m. Grass kept for pasture.
s.f. Subscription, &c. = behrī, q.v.

subs: s.m. Collection on shares of the revenue in kind
s.f. An allowance for repairing roads, &c.
s.m. The holder of a share called behrī.

n. f. 1. Welfare; good; advantage; improvement. 2. Virtue; goodness; merit; excellence.

def: adj. Absurd, vain, idle, unprofitable, fruitless; senseless, foolish, silly, stupid, nonsensical, frivolous; indecent, improper, obscene, foul, gross, immoral

adj. contract. of be-hūda, q.v.

adj. 1. Senseless; foolish; silly; stupid; nonsensical; absurd. 2. Idle; fruitless; vain; unprofitable. 3. Foul; gross; obscene; immoral; indecent; impolite. behūdah bāteṅ , n. f. Indecent language. behūdah taqrīr , n. f. Idle disputation; fiddlefaddle. behūdah uzr , n. m. An idle or absurd plea or excuse. behūdah uzr karnā , v. n. To make a vain objection. behūdah-go , n. m. A foul-mouthed fellow; a coarse or obscene talker ( bakkī ). behūdah-goī , n. f. Nonsense; obscene or idle talk ( bakwās ).
more: adj. Absurd, vain, foolish, idle, fruitless, unprofitable. بيـﮩوده گو behūda-go, A talker of nonsense, a babbler. بيـﮩوده گوٴي behūda-go-ī, s. f. Talking nonsense, nonsense.

def: n. f. Absurdity; foolishness; frivolity; nonsense; foolery.
more: s. f. Absurdity, nonsense, vanity, unprofitableness.

s. f. See بي هوشي be-hosh-ī, under بي be.

adj. 1. Without self-possession, control, or authority; incompetent. 2. Constrained; forced; compelled. be-ikhtyār , adv. 1. Nolens volens ; in spite of oneself. 2. Involuntarily; spontaneously; naturally.
n. f. Want of power; incompetency; disability; helplessness.
faithless, irreligious; dishonest, deceptive

n. f. lit . Infidelity. Irreligion; dishonesty; breach of trust; unfair dealing; unfairness. 1. Be-ímānī kā mūṅh kālā . Prov. Roguery has a black face. 2. Yā be-īmānī, terā āsrā! Prov. In fraud is my hope. be-īmānī kar'nā , v. n. 1. To play false; cheat; defraud. 2. To misappropriate; embezzle; peculate.

1. Out of place; misplaced; irrelevant. 2. Improper; unlawful; unjustifiable; illegal. 3. Unreasonable; contrary to reason; unsound; absurd. 4. Inaccurate; wrong; objectionable. be-jā , adv. Improperly; injudiciously; wrongly. be-jā kharch, yā sarf , n. m. Needless expense; extravagance; waste; dissipation.

more: n. f. H. bi two, joṛ join. Mixed corn, generally gram and barley. Saukan din din chāṅval rāṅdhe, maiṅ bejhaṛ nā khāūṅgī. Jo tauṅ bejhaṛ lāë bisā-ke, us ne bār khiṅdāūṅgī . Rus. song. My co-wife every day eats rice, I will not eat mixed grain, If you will buy me such, I vow, it out shall go again. bejhṛā , adj. Of a mixed race; mongrel.

s. f. 1. Root. 2. Origin, foundation. بيخ کن beḵẖ-kan, An extirpator, exterminator; a judge (as extirpating thieves); a thief (as destroying the subjects). بيخ کني beḵẖ-kan-ī, s. f. Extirpation, extermination, eradication.

def: adj. 1. Worthless ( birthā ). 2. Without work or employment; unemployed; idle; doing nothing. Be-kār ma-bāsh, kuchh kīyā kar. Kapṛe hī udheṛ-kar sīyā kar . Prov. Be not idle, something do. (Parodied as follows). Unrip your clothes and stitch them o'er anew. 3. Inoperative; ineffective; ineffectual; inefficient. 4. Without force; invalid; nugatory; null. be-kār karnā , v. a. 1. To thwart; bulk; frustrate; disconcert; defeat; undo. 2. To render useless or unserviceable; break; destroy. 3. To render defective; invalidate; undo.
blameless, innocent; faultless

def: s.f. Any climbing or creeping plant, creeper, climber, vine; tendril; offspring, issue, descendants; strings, rows, or festoons of leaves, &c. hung round a room or tent; embroidery, flowered tissue; cutaneous disease, eruption; — money given by the spectators to a dancing-girl, or to a player
subs: s.m. Shrubs and creepers; flowers or embroidery (on cloth, &c.): flowered border
v.n. To bear fruit, have offspring; to attain one's precise or utmost wish
v.n. A creeper to climb to the top (of); to reach the top or summit (of); to come to perfection; to flourish, prosper.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀯𑁂𑀮𑁆𑀮𑀻 (vĕllī), from Sanskrit वेल्लि (velli). Cognate with Gujarati વેલ (vel), Marathi वेल (vel), Punjabi ویل (vel) / ਵੇਲ (vel).

def: s.m. The wood-apple or Bel-tree, Ӕgle marmelos, and its fruit
more: n. f. S. वल्लि, Pr. bellī from वल्ल् to move. 1. A creeping plant; creeper; climber; parasite. Jaṛ kaṭe aur bel baṛhe . Prov. Cutting the root makes a tall tree. (Applied to eunuchs and tall persons). 2. Wooden apple; Aegle marmelos. Fac . the head. 3. Generation; offspring ( baṛhotrī ). Nanne jī kī bel! Benediction. Blest be the young ones with fair progeny! 4. A line of road marked out with a spade. 5. Tape; border. 6. Embroidery; flowered tissue. 7. A pole for directing a boat. 8. A disease; an eruption. 9. The place where the receiving-pans of a sugar-factory are placed. 10. Money given to dancing girls, players, barbers, etc. on festive occasions ( bel-baṭṭā ). 1. Kab dādā mareṅge aur kab bel baṭegī? Prov. When will the grandsire die, when will the funeral gifts be made? 2. Nācheṅ bajā-ke tālyāṅ, aur gāweṅ hījṛe, Le le-ke bel bhāo bhī batlāweṅ hījṛe . Nazīr. See now these eunuchs st…
1. (for विल्व ) s. m. The name of a fruit. (Cratæva or Ӕgle marmelos). 2. (for वल्लि ) s. f. A creeper, climber; tendril (of a vine).
s. m. The name of a flower. (Jasminum zambac). بيل پهلني bel phalne, To attain oneʼs precise or utmost wish.
subs: bel-pattar, s.m., bel-pattī, s.f. Leaf of the Bel-tree (employed as an offering to Śiva)
s.m. Fruit of the Bel-tree, wood-apple
s.f. Pulp of the Bel or wood-apple (used medicinally for diarrhœa and dysentery).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀯𑁂𑀮𑁆𑀮𑀻 (vĕllī), from Sanskrit वेल्लि (velli). Cognate with Gujarati વેલ (vel), Marathi वेल (vel), Punjabi ویل (vel) / ਵੇਲ (vel).

To be distributed (alms). belā bāṅṭ'nā , v. a. To give alms.

def: s.m. Son, boy, child; a form of address used by faqīrs towards their ćelās or disciples
more: s. m. A son, a child. بيٿاکرلينا beṭā kar lenā, To adopt a son.
subs: betā kar-lenā, v.t. To adopt a son (syn. god lenā)
s.m. A father who survives his son
s.m. (f. -ī), Grandfather; a man who has children
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi بیٿا (byṭa), from Prakrit 𑀩𑀺𑀝𑁆𑀝 (biṭṭa)., further etymology unknown. Perhaps inherited from Sanskrit बटु (baṭu, “boy, lad, chap”).

def: adj. Agitated; impatient ( be-chain ).

n. f. Agitation; restlessness. Yeh be-tābī, yeh be-khuābī, yeh be-chainī dikhātā hai, Na ghar meṅ dil merā lagtā, na sahrā mujh ko bhātā hai . On separation. Nazīr. Unquiet, sleepless, restless grown to that degree, Nor home, nor lonely wood distasteful pleaseth me.
recklessly; rashly; wildly, crazily; thoughtlessly; hysterically
wety: Derived from Classical Persian تَحَاشَى (tahāšā), from Arabic تَحَاشَى (taḥāšā, “to avoid”), with the prefix بے (be).

def: adv. A. حشی he breathed short. Recklessly; furiously; headlong. Be-tahāshā bhāgā jātā hai .
s. f. Innocence.
s.m. Sprite, goblin (especially one that is supposed to haunt cemeteries and animate corpses); a dead body occupied and animated by a goblin
more: n. m. S. वेताल A dead body occupied and animated by an evil spirit.
s. m. 1. A dead body occupied and animated by an evil spirit. 2. Name of a demon.
subs: s.f. Name of a work in Hindī (so called because it contains twenty-five tales related by a baitāl).

def: s.m. Hire, wages, pittance, reward; profession, livelihood, subsistence; silver.
s.m. = beṭnan, q.v.
adv. Badly. See be-ḍhab .
adj. Unmannerly, rude: see بيطرح be-t̤araḥ, under P. بي be.
direct plural; oblique/vocative singular
s.f. (?), Sandy unproductive soil.
s.m. The envelope in which cloth, lace, &c. purchased is folded up, and to which the purchaser is entitled; pack-cloth, wrapper.
more: s. m. The envelope, in which cloth purchased is folded up, to which the purchaser is entitled; packcloth, wrapper.

def: n. m. S. वेष्टन from वेष्ट् to surround. A pack-cloth; the envelope in which cloth, lace, etc. purchased is folded up, and to which the purchaser is entitled.
s.f. Daughter, girl
more: s. f. A daughter. بيج bīj, s. m. 1. (for वीज ) Seed. 2. (for वीर्य्य ) Sperma genitale (viri aut mulieris).
subs: (-ko), To give one's daughter (to, in marriage)
wety: Feminine of بیٹا (beṭā).

def: n. f. A daughter. 1. Ittī sī biṭyā, savā gaz kī chuṭyā . Riddle. Ans. Sūī . Needle. The daughter but a mite, her hair a yard and one-fourth quite. beṭī behoār , n. m. Intermarriage; matrimonial alliance. beṭī denā , v. n. To give one's daughter in marriage. Jis ne beṭī dī, us ne sab kuchh dīyā . Prov. Saying. Who gives his daughter gives all. beṭī roṭī , n. f. lit . Abusing a man's daughter and calling him a beggar. Calling names; foul language; abuse. beṭī roṭī karnā , v. a. To call names; abuse; insult. beṭī-wālā , n. m. beṭī-wālī , n. f. The father or mother of the bride.
direct plural of بیٹی (beṭī, “daughter”)
s.f. = biṭiyā; beṭī, qq.v. s.f. Little daughter; daughter.
oblique plural of بیٹی (beṭī, “daughter”)
vocative plural of بیٹی (beṭī, “daughter”)
oblique plural of بیٹا (beṭā, “boy; son”)
vocative plural of بیٹا (beṭā, “boy; son”)
s.m. Cane; willow
subs: s.f. Allowance for mats or wicker works for packing goods.

def: s.m. = biṭwā, q.v.
s.f. Widow ( = P. بيوه bewa, q.v.).
more: s. f. A widow. See بيوه bewa.

def: s.f. Widow ( = bewā).
more: n. f. Z. veva . A widow (بدهوا).
s. f. A widow. See بيوا bewā.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بیوَه (bēwa). Doublet of وِدَھوا (vidhavā) and بِدھوا (bidhvā).

def: adj. Immodest; shameless. Baṛā be-wahdat hai .
s.f. Widowhood ( = bewā-pā, bewā-pan)
adj. Unseasonable.
stupid; idiot
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بیوقوف (bēwuqūf), from بیـ (bē-, “without”) + Arabic وُقُوف (wuqūf, “standing still; study, knowledge”). Compare Bengali বেকুব (bekub), Gujarati બેવકૂફ (bevakūph).

def: idiocy, stupidity, foolishness; nonsense
wety: From بیوقوف (byoqof, “idiot”) + ـی (-ī).

def: alternative spelling of بے وقوفی (bevaqūfī)
wety: From بیوقوف (byoqof, “idiot”) + ـی (-ī).

def: s.m. The vehicle or self-moving car of a Hindū deity; &c. ( = bimān q.v.).
more: s. m. 1. The vehicle or self-moving car of a Hindū deity: see بمان bimān. 2. A (Hindū) bier.

def: s.m. State or situation of widowhood; widowhood.
s.m. State or situation of widowhood; widowhood.
s.m. (local) = byorā, q.v. s.m. Difference, distinction; account, explanation, history, circumstantial or detailed account ( = tafṣīl); news, tidings, intelligence; day-book
subs: byorā karnā (-kā), To relate, describe; to inform, intimate
beʼore-wār, adj. & adv. Circumstantial, detailed, explicit, distinct, clear, plain; — circumstantially, in detail, explicitly, &c. (syn. tafṣīl-wār; biʼt-tafṣīl).

def: n. m. S. हृ to take. 1. Dealing; traffic; trade; transaction. 1. Ahāre, behvāre lajjā na kāre . Prov. To eat and traffic are no shame. 2. Kāyā rākhe dharam, aur pūṅjī rākhe beohār . Prov. Religion in the body dwells, as trade in capital. 2. Business; negotiation; correspondence; intercourse. 3. Calling; trade; profession; moneylending. 4. Usage; practice; custom; procedure; manner of proceeding; course. 1. Desā desā chāl, kulā kulā beohār . Prov. The customs of countries differ as the practice of families. bevhār karnā , v. n. To negotiate; to do or transact business; treat; deal with; bargain; trade; set up in business; contract. des-bevhār , n. m. The custom of the country.
adj. 1. Wonted; customary; usual. 2. Mercantile; commercial. bevhārī , n. 1. The party engaged in any affair. 2. A trader; merchant; money lender.
s.f. Widow ( = bewa)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بِیبِی (bī-bī, “lady”).

def: nose-led
part. (of beḵẖtan; rt. Zend vij; S. vij or vić), Sifting; diffusing, dispersing (used in comp.).
speechless
adj. See s.v. be. s.m. The letter or sound b.
more: adj. Displeased ( nā-rāz ); offended; angry ( khafā ). Haq kahne se ahmaq be-zār . Prov. Truth angers fools.
adj. Displeased, angry, out of humour, disgusted: ( vulg. ) sick.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بیزَار (bēzār).

def: s. f. (dakh.) See بيزاري bezār-ī.
harmless, innoxious
wety: From Urdu بے (be, “not”) + Urdu ضرر (“harm”).

def: s. f. Anger, bad humour, displeasure.
n. prop. A Persian hero, son of (گيو) Gew, and of a sister of Rustam.
informal spelling of بے عزتی (be ‘izzatī)
s.m. The letter or sound bh.
s.f. Sudden bursting forth of flame, or steam, &c.; forcible expulsion of water from a fountain or pipe; sudden emission of stench or offensive smell.
more: n. f. H. bhāp steam. 1. A sudden burst of flame. 2. A sudden emission of stench, stink, or bad smell.

def: v.n. To be heated; to simmer, boil, bubble; to give out steam; to fume; to catch fire, to burn, be burnt or scalded; to burst into flame, blaze up suddenly; to be enraged or exasperated, fly into a passion, to rush on with rage; to run with great speed (a horse); to roar (as a tiger); to be poured out, gush forth: — bhabak-jānā = bhabaknā.
more: v. n. 1. To become hot; to be heated, overboiled. 2. To simmer; boil; give out steam. 3. To burst into flame; blaze up suddenly. Bārūd bhabak uṭṭhī . 4. To be burnt, scalded. Hin. Hāth bhabhak gayā . 5. To flare up; fire up; rage; fret and fume; turn red with anger. 6. To roar; thunder. Sher bhabkā . 7. To be poured out, thrown away copiously. E.
v. n. 1. To be enraged, to rush on with rage. 2. To catch fire. 3. To run with great rapidity (a horse).

def: s.m. A grass of which rope is made; a species of nettle; — light black soil; — name of a large forest at the foot of the Sewalik hills.
more: n. m. 1. Light black soil. 2. A grass of which rope is made.

def: s.m. = bhabbhar, q.v.
more: n. m. H. bhaṛbhaṛ a confused noise. 1. Noise; tumult; uproar. 2. Panic; flight; stampede. bhabbaṛ paṛnā , v. n. 1. To be panic-struck. 2. To take flight.

def: adj. Fat, corpulent.
s.f. = bhabak, q.v. s.f. Sudden bursting forth of flame, or steam, &c.; forcible expulsion of water from a fountain or pipe; sudden emission of stench or offensive smell.
more: s. f. 1. Sudden bursting forth of flame. 2. Forcible expulsion of water from a fountain or pipe. 3. Smell arising suddenly.

def: v.n. = bhabaknā, q.v. v.n. To be heated; to simmer, boil, bubble; to give out steam; to fume; to catch fire, to burn, be burnt or scalded; to burst into flame, blaze up suddenly; to be enraged or exasperated, fly into a passion, to rush on with rage; to run with great speed (a horse); to roar (as a tiger); to be poured out, gush forth: — bhabak-jānā = bhabaknā.
more: v. n. 1. To simmer, to bubble. 2. To emit steam, to fume, to boil.

def: s.m. Disquietude, solicitude ( = khaṭkā), dread, alarm; panic, stampede; tumult, uproar
more: s. m. Solicitude, alarm. بهبهرپڙنا bhabhar-paṛnā, v. n. To be alarmed, to be frightened.
subs: (-meṅ), To be alarmed; to be panic-stricken; to take to flight.

def: v.n. To be solicitous; to be alarmed or frightened, to take fright (cf. bhabhar paṛnā).
more: v. n. To be solicitous, to be alarmed, to be frightened.

ety: S. भ्रातृ+वधू; or H. bhāʼī+bī = bī-bī
def: s.f. Brother's wife, sister-in-law (syn. bhāwaj, bhaujāʼī, bhaujī).
v.n. To swell (particularly the face = bharbharānā, q.v.); to feel doubt or solicitude, &c. ( = bhabharnā, q.v.).
more: v. n. To swell (particularly, the face). See بهربهرانا bharbharānā.

def: adj. Flaming, blazing, ablaze; red (as a live coal); white, shining, bright, glowing, refulgent, splendid, beautiful; — s.m. Blaze, flame; explosion
more: 1. s. m. Blaze, flame, explosion. 2. adj. Red (as a coal, sometimes applied to the splendour of a beloved personʼs countenance); splendid, beautiful.
subs: v.n. To turn red (or livid) with rage.

def: s.f. Ashes of cowdung, &c. which Hindū devotees rub on their bodies (in imitation of Śiva); ashes from a shrine with which Hindū worshippers smear their foreheads.
more: s. f. Ashes of cow-dung which faḳīrs rub over their bodies.

def: adj. = bhāvī, q.v. s.m. Future time, the future
subs: adj. Dependent on, or subject to, the future.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi بھابی (bhaby /⁠bhābī⁠/) (c. 1697) and Middle Hindi بھابھی (bhabhy /⁠bhābhī⁠/), from Prakrit *𑀪𑀸𑀩𑁆𑀪 (*bhābbha), from baby talk. Cognate with Punjabi بھابی (bhābī).

ety: S. भ्रातृ+वधू; or H. bhāʼī+bī = bī-bī
def: s.f. Brother's wife, sister-in-law (syn. bhāwaj, bhaujāʼī, bhaujī).
more: n. f. S. भातूबधू, H. bhā , Cont. of bhāī brother, bī , Cont. of bahū wife. Brother's wife; sister-in-law (on the brother's side).
adj. Future, about to be, predestined.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi بھابی (bhaby /⁠bhābī⁠/) (c. 1697) and Middle Hindi بھابھی (bhabhy /⁠bhābhī⁠/), from Prakrit *𑀪𑀸𑀩𑁆𑀪 (*bhābbha), from baby talk. Cognate with Punjabi بھابی (bhābī).

def: direct plural of بھابی (bhābī, “sister-in-law (brother's (or friend's) wife)”)
oblique plural of بھابی (bhābī, “sister-in-law (brother's (or friend's) wife)”)
vocative plural of بھابی (bhābī, “sister-in-law (brother's (or friend's) wife)”)
s.m. Blast from a furnace; alembic, still; — a kind of drinking vessel with a large mouth.
more: n. m. 1. A still. 2. A blast from a furnace.
s. m. 1. (from بهاپهـ Sans. वाष्प ) An alembick, a still. 2. A kind of drinking vessel with a large mouth.

def: v.t. To heat, kindle, light; to cause to boil, &c.; to cause to blaze up suddenly; to provoke, enrage, exasperate; to excite, stimulate; to spur a horse; &c. (see bhabaknā).
more: (caus. of بهبکنا) v. a. 1. To kindle, to light. 2. To provoke, to exasperate, to enrage. 3. To spur a horse.

def: s.f. Threat, menace.
more: n. f. Threat; menace; intimidation. bandar yā gīdaṛ bhabkī , n. f. The threatening look of a monkey or jackal. Ye bandar bhabkī kisī aur ko denā .

def: s.f. = bhabhūt, q.v. s.f. Ashes of cowdung, &c. which Hindū devotees rub on their bodies (in imitation of Śiva); ashes from a shrine with which Hindū worshippers smear their foreheads.
adj. = bhabhūkā, q.v. adj. Flaming, blazing, ablaze; red (as a live coal); white, shining, bright, glowing, refulgent, splendid, beautiful; — s.m. Blaze, flame; explosion
more: adj. 1. Red (as coal). 2. White; shining; bright; refulgent; most beautiful. Kuchh āṅkhoṅ meṅ narmī, woh sādā sā ḍhaṅg, Woh haṅstā sā mukhṛā, bhabūkā sā raṅg . Sohan Lāl. bhabūkā honā , v. n. To turn red with rage. Ye sun-kar woh sholā bhabūkā huī, Lagī kahne, haë haë! balā kyā huī! Mīr Hasan.
subs: v.n. To turn red (or livid) with rage.

def: s.f. = bhabhūt, q.v. s.f. Ashes of cowdung, &c. which Hindū devotees rub on their bodies (in imitation of Śiva); ashes from a shrine with which Hindū worshippers smear their foreheads.
more: n. f. S. बिभूति. Ashes from a shrine rubbed on their foreheads by Hindū worshippers; ashes from their own fires which ascetics or jogīs rub over their bodies. bhabūt ramānā, malnā, yā lagānā , v. n. To rub ashes over the body.

def: s.f. = bhabhūt, q.v. s.f. Ashes of cowdung, &c. which Hindū devotees rub on their bodies (in imitation of Śiva); ashes from a shrine with which Hindū worshippers smear their foreheads.

subs: v.n. To be astonished, amazed, staggered, &c.

more: v. n. To be astonished or amazed.
subs: v.n. To be astonished, amazed, staggered, &c.

s.m. = bhakshaṇ, q.v. s.m. Eating, feeding, devouring, gormandizing; food, meal, victuals
subs: (-ko), To eat, feed upon, devour; to take a meal; to gormandize.

def: s.m. = bhakshak, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Eating, devouring, feeding upon; voracious, gluttonous; — eater, feeder, devourer, glutton.
more: n. m. One who devours; a destroyer. Jākā Rām rachchhak, tākā kaun bhachchak? Prov. Whom Ram protects no one can slay.
